tags.name: `mc-pagination`
tags.description:
Purpose: To provide navigation for paginated content.
Category: Navigation, Data Display
When to use: When you need to navigate through a set of paginated content.
When not to use: When you have a single page of content or don't need pagination.
Works with components: mc-table, mc-list, mc-list-item, mc-card, mc-drawer, mc-modal
Alternative component names: Pagination Control, Page Navigator, Page Selector, Page Controller, Page Switcher

Events:

  * ``pagechange`` {`CustomEvent<number>`} - Emitted when the page is changed.

  * ``pagesizechange`` {`CustomEvent<number>`} - Emitted when the rows per page value is changed.

  * `pagechange` {`CustomEvent<number>`} - 

  * `pagesizechange` {`CustomEvent<number>`} - 

Slots:

  * ` ` {} - The default slot for the router links

Attributes:

  * `labels` {`Partial<IMcPaginationLabels> | undefined`} - Labels object for customizing component text.
If not provided, labels are automatically loaded based on document's lang attribute.

  * `totalpages` {`number`} - Number of total pages.

  * `totalrecords` {`number`} - Total number of rows across all pages.
Required when `showpagestatus` is enabled to display accurate row count (e.g., "1-10 of 75 rows").
If not provided when `showpagestatus` is true, the status will show a simplified format without the total (e.g., "1-10").

  * `currentpage` {`number`} - Number of the selected page.

  * `hiddenlabels` {`boolean`} - If set to `true` or the attribute is just presented without any value,
then the `previous` and `next` label will not appear,
but will be used as `aria-label` only

  * `showlabels` {`boolean`} - If set to `true` or the attribute is just presented without any value,
then the `previous`, `next`, `first`, and `last` labels will appear,
By default, labels for those buttons are hidden but are used as `aria-label`

  * `visiblepages` {`number`} - Maximum number of visible pages to be displayed.
If the `disabledtruncation` is set to `false` the dots used to truncate the pages will also be counted.
When set to `0` only navigation buttons will be displayed.

  * `previouslabel` {`string`} - Label for the `previous` button.
The value will also be used as aria-label on the button.

  * `nextlabel` {`string`} - Label for the `next` button.
The value will also be used as aria-label on the button.

  * `arialabel` {`string`} - The `aria-label` for the pagination wrapper

  * `fit` {`Fit`} - Fit of the pagination.

  * `disabledtruncation` {`boolean`} - If set to `true` or the attribute is just presented without any value,
then three dots will not be used to truncate the pages and first and last page navigation will not be visible.

  * `variant` {`PaginationVariant | undefined`} - Variant of the pagination component.
- 'button': Standard pagination with numbered page buttons (1, 2, 3...), recommended to use for content pagination (e.g., search results and articles)
- 'select': Advanced pagination with page selector dropdown instead of numbered buttons, recommended to use for tables with less than 300 rows
- 'input': Minimal pagination with only navigation arrows (first/prev/next/last), recommended to use for tables with more than 300 rows

  * `showfirstandlastpage` {`boolean`} - If set to `true`, displays first and last navigation buttons (double arrows) in addition to prev/next buttons.

  * `showpagesize` {`boolean`} - If set to `true`, displays a rows per page selector dropdown in the start section.
Can be combined with any variant.

  * `showpagestatus` {`boolean`} - If set to `true`, displays a status text showing the current range of items in the start section.
When `totalrecords` is provided: shows "1-10 of 75 rows"
When `totalrecords` is not provided: shows simplified format "1-10"
Can be combined with any variant.

  * `pagesizeoptions` {`number[]`} - Data for the rows per page selector dropdown options as an array of numbers.

  * `pagesize` {`number`} - Currently selected rows per page value.

Properties:

  * `_currentPage` {`number`} - 

  * `_totalpages` {`number`} - 

  * `_totalrecords` {`number`} - 

  * `_linkElements` {`HTMLElement[]`} - 

  * `currentFocusIndex` {`number | undefined`} - 

  * `hasLinkSlot` {`boolean`} - 

  * `linkSlotElements` {`HTMLElement[]`} - 

  * `inputElement` - 

  * `computedLabels` {`Required<IMcPaginationLabels>`} - 

  * `pagesData` {`IMcSelectData[]`} - 

  * `rowsPerPageSelectData` {`IMcSelectData[]`} - 

  * `labels` {`Partial<IMcPaginationLabels> | undefined`} - Labels object for customizing component text.
If not provided, labels are automatically loaded based on document's lang attribute.

  * `totalpages` {`number`} - Number of total pages.

  * `totalrecords` {`number`} - Total number of rows across all pages.
Required when `showpagestatus` is enabled to display accurate row count (e.g., "1-10 of 75 rows").
If not provided when `showpagestatus` is true, the status will show a simplified format without the total (e.g., "1-10").

  * `currentpage` {`number`} - Number of the selected page.

  * `hiddenlabels` {`boolean`} - If set to `true` or the attribute is just presented without any value,
then the `previous` and `next` label will not appear,
but will be used as `aria-label` only

  * `showlabels` {`boolean`} - If set to `true` or the attribute is just presented without any value,
then the `previous`, `next`, `first`, and `last` labels will appear,
By default, labels for those buttons are hidden but are used as `aria-label`

  * `visiblepages` {`number`} - Maximum number of visible pages to be displayed.
If the `disabledtruncation` is set to `false` the dots used to truncate the pages will also be counted.
When set to `0` only navigation buttons will be displayed.

  * `previouslabel` {`string`} - Label for the `previous` button.
The value will also be used as aria-label on the button.

  * `nextlabel` {`string`} - Label for the `next` button.
The value will also be used as aria-label on the button.

  * `arialabel` {`string`} - The `aria-label` for the pagination wrapper

  * `fit` {`Fit`} - Fit of the pagination.

  * `disabledtruncation` {`boolean`} - If set to `true` or the attribute is just presented without any value,
then three dots will not be used to truncate the pages and first and last page navigation will not be visible.

  * `variant` {`PaginationVariant | undefined`} - Variant of the pagination component.
- 'button': Standard pagination with numbered page buttons (1, 2, 3...), recommended to use for content pagination (e.g., search results and articles)
- 'select': Advanced pagination with page selector dropdown instead of numbered buttons, recommended to use for tables with less than 300 rows
- 'input': Minimal pagination with only navigation arrows (first/prev/next/last), recommended to use for tables with more than 300 rows

  * `showfirstandlastpage` {`boolean`} - If set to `true`, displays first and last navigation buttons (double arrows) in addition to prev/next buttons.

  * `showpagesize` {`boolean`} - If set to `true`, displays a rows per page selector dropdown in the start section.
Can be combined with any variant.

  * `showpagestatus` {`boolean`} - If set to `true`, displays a status text showing the current range of items in the start section.
When `totalrecords` is provided: shows "1-10 of 75 rows"
When `totalrecords` is not provided: shows simplified format "1-10"
Can be combined with any variant.

  * `pagesizeoptions` {`number[]`} - Data for the rows per page selector dropdown options as an array of numbers.

  * `pagesize` {`number`} - Currently selected rows per page value.

  * `pagechange` - Page number changed

  * `pagesizechange` - Rows per page value changed

  * `langObserver` {`MutationObserver | undefined`} - 

  * `handleDocumentLangChange` - Request update when document lang changes
